fields.description:
## Why

A core driver of the visual-editing goal is letting developers ADD / REMOVE individual criteria
(prompts) and see/adjust their inclusion conditions (the `if:` overlay predicate) and the batch
grouping. The editor must understand the genuinely-new `batch` step, and render/edit the EXISTING
`if:` predicate (overlays adopt `if:` — no new field). Scope is bounded to these, not a general canvas.

## What

Extend the workflow visual editor (docs/workflow-editor.md) to render and edit the `batch` step (its
group/budget/ladder params) and the `if:` overlay predicate on a prompt step, including ADD/REMOVE of a
conditionally-included prompt. CONFIRM whether the editor already renders `if:` (it is an existing
schema field) and extend only as needed. Preserve the engine invariants: clean git diffs +
schema-validated text round-trip (the e6d9 editor invariant). The demo workflow is the refinement sample.

## Scope

- The workflow editor surface (bpmn-js / editor assets + the Python render/round-trip; see
  docs/workflow-editor.md + tests/e2e/test_editor_*).
- Render + edit the `batch` step and the `if:` overlay predicate; add/remove a conditionally-included prompt.
- Round-trip keeps clean git diffs + schema validation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The editor renders/edits the `batch` step (group/budget/ladder) and a prompt step's `if:` overlay
      predicate, and supports ADD/REMOVE of a conditionally-included prompt.
- [ ] Editor round-trip preserves clean git diffs + schema-validated text (the e6d9 invariant).
- [ ] An editor test (tests/e2e or equivalent) covers rendering/editing the `batch` step and the `if:` predicate.
- [ ] make lint + the relevant editor/workflow tests pass.
